textPlain: Th article doesn't clarify... Are the "dangerous illegals [...] rampaging the streets" meant to be immigrants or ICE agents?
The best time to quit Spotify was years ago when they gave Joe Rogan a huge paycheck. The next best time is now.
Ek would sell his mom for profit.

This is one of (though not the only) reasons I'm uncomfortable having my music on Spotify.I do still publish there, but I vastly prefer my fans grab my music via IPFS or Bit Torrent.It's so bizarre that someone hovering over the "play" button on one of my songs doesn't know whether they're about to hear Harry's mandolin or an authoritarian recruiting pitch.

There are plenty of alternatives. Time to cancel your subscriptions and remember Spotify's decisions once (if ever) the US government regains its senses. What ICE is doing is disgusting and what Trump is doing with ICE is scary.

I mean, yea. Organizations will follow the hand that feeds them. You might as well be surprised that Israel is bombing civilians during a supposed ceasefire, or that SNAP benefits end Nov 1st. We just live in a horrifyingly shitty place.
I cancelled over it. Not worth it to support a company that is willing to profit from ICE.

There's already an artist-led movement to boycott Spotify, if you were looking for motivation to move your stuff to a competitor like Bandcamp or Tidal. This article is not about the ICE advertisements.https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2025/oct/12/spotify-b...
ditto. my friends can't even play my song even if i sent a link to my profile
